tldr: Headline coverage of the Philippines building collapse in Angeles City is dominated by a running casualty tally — dead, missing, and trapped — with virtually every international outlet leading on the numbers. Wire services and most regional outlets frame the story as a live death toll, while South African, Reuters, and Hong Kong headlines pivot a day later to rescue urgency ('racing against time'). Only Philippine domestic outlets humanise the disaster, surfacing child vulnerability and a survivor's first-person account; the safety-violation backstory present in body text is entirely absent from any headline.

frames.sub_frame: Dead, missing, and trapped worker counts

evidence.quote: Philippines: Deadly building collapse traps workers
Morocco building collapse kills at least nine
Death toll rises to four in Philippines building collapse
21 people trapped under rubble after deadly building collapse
Philippines: 20 feared trapped after building collapse
3 dead, 17 still missing in collapse of unfinished hotel
Philippine rescuers race to free workers trapped
Survivors: Kids were inside Angeles City site
Angeles City building collapse: Worker recounts narrow

bottom_line: Headline coverage reduces the Philippines building collapse almost entirely to a casualty tally — dead, missing, trapped — with Philippine outlets uniquely humanising the disaster through survivor testimony and child vulnerability.
